Dear Songwriters, Composers and Publishers:
The value of performances from certain sources has been enhanced by revenues from General Licensing.
These revenues represent an important source of income for you, and include fees from many sources such
as restaurants, bars, hotels, retail stores, and theme parks.
Important Information About Your U.S. Earnings
BMI distributes all royalties received in accordance with each year’s surveyed performances after deducting
operating expenses.  If you believe there has been an error or omission in your statement, please notify your
usual BMI Creative office or representative, IN WRITING, of the perceived omission or error.  Your written
notice of perceived error or omission in a particular statement must be received by BMI within nine months of
the mailing date of that statement, and any adjustment will be limited to that statement.  Requests for
adjustments for prior years are an unfair burden on current performance royalty distributions.  BMI will not
consider any adjustment requests unless they are received within the nine-month period specified above.
Important Information About Your International Earnings
BMI distributes all international royalties in accordance with BMI’s policies and with information given to BMI
by the respective foreign societies, after deducting a general administration charge of currently 3.6% for
operating expenses.  If you believe there has been an error or omission in your statement, please notify your
usual BMI Creative office or representative, IN WRITING, of the perceived omission or error.  Your written
notice of perceived error or omission in a particular statement must be received by BMI within nine months of
the mailing date of that statement, and any adjustments will be limited to that statement.  BMI has the right not
to consider any adjustment requests unless they are received within the nine-month period specified above.
Please note that foreign societies have limited the period for which they will accept requests for adjustments
for missing or incorrect payments.  It is, therefore, very important that you study your foreign royalty statement
immediately upon receipt, and that you give BMI detailed information about incorrect or missing payments so
BMI may initiate an inquiry to the foreign societies.  Delays in notifying BMI may cause you to forego the
opportunity to receive an adjustment.  Please also note that due to the extensive research involved in
preparing inquiries to the foreign societies and the research time it takes the foreign society to review the
documentation, foreign adjustments may take many months to bring to resolution.

Dear Songwriters, Composers and Publishers:
The value of performances from certain sources has been enhanced by revenues from General Licensing.
These revenues represent an important source of income for you, and include fees from many sources such
as restaurants, bars, hotels, retail stores, and theme parks.
Important Information About Your U.S. Earnings
BMI distributes all royalties received in accordance with each year’s surveyed performances after deducting
operating expenses.  If you believe there has been an error or omission in your statement, please notify your
usual BMI Creative office or representative, IN WRITING, of the perceived omission or error.  Your written
notice of perceived error or omission in a particular statement must be received by BMI within nine months of
the mailing date of that statement, and any adjustment will be limited to that statement.  Requests for
adjustments for prior years are an unfair burden on current performance royalty distributions.  BMI will not
consider any adjustment requests unless they are received within the nine-month period specified above.
Important Information About Your International Earnings
BMI distributes all international royalties in accordance with BMI’s policies and with information given to BMI
by the respective foreign societies, after deducting a general administration charge of currently 3.6% for
operating expenses.  If you believe there has been an error or omission in your statement, please notify your
usual BMI Creative office or representative, IN WRITING, of the perceived omission or error.  Your written
notice of perceived error or omission in a particular statement must be received by BMI within nine months of
the mailing date of that statement, and any adjustments will be limited to that statement.  BMI has the right not
to consider any adjustment requests unless they are received within the nine-month period specified above.
Please note that foreign societies have limited the period for which they will accept requests for adjustments
for missing or incorrect payments.  It is, therefore, very important that you study your foreign royalty statement
immediately upon receipt, and that you give BMI detailed information about incorrect or missing payments so
BMI may initiate an inquiry to the foreign societies.  Delays in notifying BMI may cause you to forego the
opportunity to receive an adjustment.  Please also note that due to the extensive research involved in
preparing inquiries to the foreign societies and the research time it takes the foreign society to review the
documentation, foreign adjustments may take many months to bring to resolution.
